essays.title: How are the digital nomads living in Canggu, Bali
essays.excerpt: The main reason for staying in Canggu for a few days is to see how digital nomads from all over the world are living, what they are doing to make money, and whether this lifestyle is feasible for themselves? People in this community usually wake up around 4 in the morning to go surfing, and then someone may have started working downstairs by 8 o'clock. They go surfing again around 6 pm in the evening ..
essays.content:
The main reason for staying in Canggu for a few days is to see how digital nomads from all over the world are living, what they are doing to make money, and whether this lifestyle is feasible for themselves?

I sat down there waiting for the bus. The European girl on the left had been on the phone with her friends for a while, with a faint cry in her voice. She said that she needed to turn on her camera for remote work, and even worked longer hours. She didn't know why she had to do this and felt a deep sense of loneliness here. The German guy diagonally across from me said to the guy next to me, 'He scraped himself,' and began to talk about what he had encountered. It seems that everyone's feelings are similar. They will encounter work pressure and choose a remote job that seems to perfectly solve all pain points, but eventually become disillusioned and new problems arise one after another.

essays.title: On a rainy day in early summer, remember to visit Tiger Hill
essays.excerpt: Wake up in the morning with water. I am very conflicted. This morning, I wanted to see how others wrote about Tiger Hill. Seeing some of the things they wrote, I developed a disdain for the word "Wenqing". I think most of their feelings are groundless, one-sided, artificial, and even self deprecating. This is also the reason why I have been trying to hide my love for reading and writing for many years, and I have felt that literature is important since middle school ..
essays.content:
Wake up in the morning with water. I am very conflicted. This morning, I wanted to see how others wrote about Tiger Hill. Seeing some of the things they wrote, I developed a disdain for the word "Wenqing". I think most of their feelings are groundless, one-sided, artificial, and even self deprecating.

I woke up very early this morning. During the days when I didn't go to work, I couldn't use my alarm clock anymore. After learning to be good, I would consciously put down my phone, go wash up, and then obediently lie down in my room. Then I woke up around 7 or 8 o'clock by myself. Of course, there were also cases where I woke up around 3 or 4 o'clock a few days ago and couldn't sleep again.

In 2500 years, anything can happen. From the 2500 year old Tiger Hill, the modern high-rise buildings in the industrial park are clearly visible. Once you enter the palace where the king of Wu left 1000 years ago, the imperial mausoleum, the Buddhist holy land, and one of the "five mountains and ten temples", you enter a completely different field. It seems that you have entered another time and space. You and countless people have looked up at the solemn Lingyan Temple Tower together for 2500 years, stopped at the same 1000 person stone with the former generals, princes, literati and poets, watched the same kind of flowers on the Tiger Hill, carefully looked at each table and chair, each piece of inscription, sword pool, flowing water, pavilions, towers and towers. Time and space overlap, just like the essence left over from previous generations is presented to you in a flat way, still Enjoy. Chinese aesthetics is a kind of temperament.

essays.content:
I think there are several main reasons why Islam is so prevalent in Southeast Asia:

For the sake of those jade, he gave up his main mission of pilgrimage; For the sake of those jade, he abandoned his wife and children; For the sake of those jade, he buried the Ice Jade mother and daughter... He was always driven by jade throughout his life, and if it weren't for it, he might not have walked through every step like this. The road of life can no longer be turned back, and he sees all the jade of life as lost.

Originally, Zheng He returned. On the deck, warriors are wearing armor and holding halberds, and the strangely dressed guide looks ahead, comparing his hands and drawing as if telling the dangers of the route. Beside him, a heroic man in a red robe stands tall at the bow of the ship, holding a compass in his left hand and pointing to the sea and sky in his right hand. This is Zheng He, known for his seven voyages to the West. The screen is a silent history, but reading it makes one's soul flutter, as if hearing the earth shattering sound of waves and feeling the chilling sea breeze. ”

|Dimension | Zheng He's voyages to the West | Columbus/Vasco da Gama voyage|

essays.title: Southeast Asia Tour - Ho Chi Minh City in Vietnam and the Saigon in My Memory
essays.excerpt: Seeing that my friends on my social media are busy and constantly updating, I decided to take note while my memories are still fresh. I arrived at Tan Son Nhat International Airport in Ho Chi Minh City at 17:00 in the afternoon. I went to the window with my passport and electronic visa photo to exchange for another paper visa smoothly. I waited for about 2 minutes and no one queued up. What I criticize is the customs, they are all artificial windows, and people all over the world are treated equally ..
essays.content:
Seeing that my friends on my social media are busy and constantly updating, I decided to take note while my memories are still fresh.

To put it superficially, my limited knowledge of Saigon comes from watching Duras' "Lover" several times in high school. This experience and the exchange with the locals have given me a real sense of the complexity and harmony that blends Vietnamese local culture, Confucian culture, French culture left over from the colonial period, American culture, Japanese culture, and more. It's like a damp oil painting, with an elegant and trendy side, as well as a side that makes me feel extremely dilapidated and backward, interesting yet boring.

Citywalk in Ubud, go hiking, yoga, watching a performance at the Ubud Palace, and learning what a self-contained energy field is ..
essays.content:
_April 25th, 2025 23:21_

My mother is gentle, kind, and well behaved. She is beautiful and charming, but only they are not favored. Fate seems to have never favored them. When they were young, they were surrounded by these few people, and now they are still these few people. Some people come and go.

essays.title: Journey to Southeast Asia - Islam
essays.excerpt: _April 28, 2025 01:31_ In my inherent understanding, Islam is only concentrated in the Middle East, North Africa, and regions such as Xinjiang, Ningxia, and Gansu in China, mostly derived from abstract high school history and geography books. When I arrived in Southeast Asia, I found that Muslims were everywhere, on the streets, in shops, in shopping malls, at stations, and at airports, all filled with bags ..
essays.content:
_April 28, 2025 at 01:31_

About 87% of the population in Indonesia adheres to Islam, with a Muslim population of 200 million, making it the country with the largest Muslim population in the world. It can be said that the power in the Islamic world is definitely the strongest. However, due to its geographical location, many people tend to overlook this Islamic powerhouse. Perhaps due to the secularization of this country, after all, it is a separation of church and state, and although Islam has a significant influence, it has not yet controlled the state power.

|Indonesia | Islam (non state religion) | Moderates dominate, diversity coexists | Conflict between Islamic law and secular law in Aceh|

essays.title: Southeast Asia Tour - Surabaya Volcano Guide
essays.excerpt: _April 24, 2025 00:04_ How deficient am I in dopamine? In the afternoon, my whole life was hopeless, and in the evening, I went for a 2km run. My mobility switched painlessly and returned to normal. This trip was in Surabaya, and I found a group to visit Bromo and Ijen volcanoes. The Indonesian name for Surabaya, also known as Surabaya, follows the locals ..
essays.content:
_April 24, 2025 00:04_

What does this mean? He's just the kind of little smug smile that seduces people, looking up at me and saying 'Darling'. I said hahaha, Darling, Sweetheart and so on. He said you can go back and talk to your boyfriend. I could only smile and didn't dare to say anything. I really feel like there are no cultural differences. Then Shuangshuang interacted with us again and taught me how to say 'suka'. They told me that it means' I love you '. Shuangshuang is a 99 year old girl who was sent to Surabaya and taught herself a lot of Indonesian. She took us to an Indonesian restaurant to order dishes in Indonesian, as if someone was covering up that feeling.

He took out his phone and showed me a video of him playing volleyball, which was a very handsome smash. I played it twice in a row, and I felt like he was mesmerized by himself. Then I was very surprised to say to everyone, wow, his hobby is volleyball, very handsome spike, and they followed suit. I asked him how many times he plays volleyball a week, and he looked up for a moment. He said it's been a long time since he last played volleyball, and the light in his eyes has dimmed a bit. I asked him if he was still in school, but he just didn't reply to me, just bowing his head.

essays.title: The essence of wealth
essays.excerpt: The essence of wealth is "survival redundancy". Human pursuit of huge wealth is essentially a pursuit of certainty about the future. In biology, this is called 'energy storage'; In psychology, this is called 'sense of security'; In the financial market, this is called 'marginal safety'.
essays.content: The essence of wealth is' survival redundancy '

essays.title: Looking back at these ten years, written at the end of the lunar calendar 2024
essays.excerpt: I wanted to write a lot, but suddenly something opened up an emotional gap. When I woke up this morning, I felt a bit emotional. I looked at some damaged video clips on my phone that were not saved properly, and some of them kept pulling me back to that moment. In other words, I want to escape into that past time and time again. Today I want to know why and what exactly it is ..
essays.content:
I wanted to write a lot, but suddenly something opened up an emotional gap

In the past year, I have encountered many difficulties, and I have also seen the crux of the problem that has been trapping me and making me unconsciously retreat. I always remember someone mentioning that you can't help avoiding people. Similarly, most likely speaking of me, I saw at the time, like a surprise, someone pointed out my problem to the point. A point that I want to define but have been unable to grasp. Later on, I spent some time in the world to become aware of myself, chatting with psychological counselors, and reading about positive psychology. Every time I make a decision about what to do, I realize this milestone and try to be brave enough to keep moving forward instead of running away.

Returning to why my teacher said my way of thinking is very Western, I looked back and realized that it may be because I chose to break away from my environment when I was very young. I stubbornly began to educate myself with what I read in books. Naturally, I have no interest in the philosophy of Confucianism in China. Of course, I like Zhuangzi, but I don't know why I embraced Western philosophy. For example, a classmate told me about age and the selection in the marriage market, and the influence of customs on their marriage and childbearing. For people in their area, it seems like a standard practice, but these things seem to have never appeared in my underlying code. I said I don't understand why this is the case. I can understand that it was the right choice, but I don't understand why. This could be a form of freedom for me, or it could become a regret.

essays.title: Southeast Asia trip: I want to buy tickets and apply for a visa today, and fly directly the next day
essays.excerpt: _April 2, 2025 00:17_ _ Indonesia _ "I don't understand my hometown because I don't have a reference. I don't understand my hometown, I don't understand my past way of life. But when I leave my hometown, when I have been away for a long time, I will miss my hometown. Only then can I begin to understand my hometown, understand the kind of people who are different from each other
essays.content:
_April 2, 2025 00:17_

I said my level of socialization lags far behind my peers. When I first started school, my roommates had already figured out where to take the civil service exam, who to date, who to date, when to get married, and when to have children. However, my mind was filled with confusion. My roommate asked me why there seemed to be no social clock, and I didn't know why they thought it was unreasonable from a rational perspective, while also feeling that they had no choice.

Due to being forced to leave their hometown and homeland, they cherish and strive to maintain the traditions in their bloodline even more. When I arrived in Malaysia, I seemed to only know the origin of our nation. In mainland China, these cultural markers that seem to have been destroyed and erased by some human factors, modernization, and industrialization processes.

essays.title: Safe Evacuation from the 21st Century: A brief dash into my ideal teenage years
essays.excerpt: On Friday, my condition improved a lot, mainly due to the energy gained from chatting with friends last night, as well as keeping my hardcore exercise for a while last night, which secreted enough dopamine for a long time. In the evening, I watched another movie called 'Safe Evacuation from the 21st Century' and watched an interview with Zhang Ruoyun and Yi Lijing, which brought back some dusty memories of my youth. I thought these things were early ..
essays.content:
On Friday, my condition improved a lot, mainly due to the energy gained from chatting with friends last night, as well as keeping my hardcore exercise for a while last night, which secreted enough dopamine for a long time.

As I grew older, my sense of time became extremely blurred, and I couldn't distinguish whether something happened on Thursday or Friday. Even though it was only Saturday today, I couldn't remember where I went a year ago, what I did in the past three months, and what I did in the past six months. In fact, I didn't have much memory of it, and it didn't seem important. And some memories are as clear as fragments from yesterday, upon careful calculation, they turned out to be ten years ago.
